# Package Installation


```{r global-setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r set-environment-dependencies}
required_packages <- c(
  "tidyr", "dplyr", "tibble", "purrr", "moments", "quantmod", "reticulate", "httr", "jsonlite", "xts", "readr", "stringr", "ggplot2", "plotly", "e1071", "tseries", "pracma", "htmltools", "zoo", "viridisLite", "lubridate", "ggcorrplot", "ggnetwork", "igraph", "gridExtra", "kableExtra", "psych", "urca", "broom", "tidyverse", "TDA", "webshot2", "urca", "fUnitRoots"

)

missing_packages <- required_packages[!sapply(required_packages, requireNamespace, quietly = TRUE)]

if (length(missing_packages) > 0) {
  cat("Installing missing dependencies:", paste(missing_packages, collapse = ", "), "\n")
  install.packages(missing_packages)
}

invisible(lapply(required_packages, library, character.only = TRUE))
```


# Global Variables

Set up the following dates:
```{r set-date}
start_date <- as.Date("2019-12-01")
end_date <- as.Date("2025-12-31")
```


```{r set-names}
# Resolve the e1071 vs moments function conflict explicitly
# We enforce the 'moments' package versions for standard econometric properties
skewness <- moments::skewness
kurtosis <- moments::kurtosis

# Explicitly instantiate the pipe operator if tidyverse initialization glitched
`%>%` <- magistr_pipe <- dplyr::`%>%`

# Verify baseline configurations to silence downstream warnings
options(xts.warn_dplyr_breaks_lag = FALSE)
```
